target.name: Mission Zero Technologies
target.founder: Nicholas Chadwick, Shiladitya Ghosh, Gaël Gobaille-Shaw

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Mission Zero Technologies is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Nicholas Chadwick, Shiladitya Ghosh, and Gaël Gobaille-Shaw that has raised £21.8M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of nine private companies and one public comparable in the climate and energy sector, with primary reliance on same-stage, same-sector private comps including Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy.

The peer-median method applied across three directly comparable Series A climate and energy companies yields a central valuation of $150M. The peer set ranges from $110M (Steady Energy) to $200M (Graphyte), establishing boundaries for the analysis. This method represents the applicable triangulation approach given available data; broader sector multiples or revenue-based methods are not supported by the provided dataset. The single peer-median result implies a $150M valuation with a floor at $110M and ceiling at $200M across the comparable set.

The triangulated range supporting this analysis is $128M (low) to $172M (high) with a median of $150M. Key uncertainties center on the representativeness of the three-company peer set—particularly whether Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy track Mission Zero's technology maturity, customer traction, and capital efficiency—and the limited data set precludes confidence interval narrowing. Additional methods anchored to revenue multiples, venture capital method, or discounted cash flow analysis would strengthen conviction but cannot be applied without underlying financial metrics.
